methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
DefSecIntel, a Series A-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Jaanus Tamm, is evaluated against a comp universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

The peer-median method applies directly given DefSecIntel's alignment with same-sector, same-stage comparables. The median valuation across four primary peers—Scale AI ($29.0B), Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yields $400M. This method produces a wide range of $150M to $29.0B, reflecting material heterogeneity in the sample; Scale AI's outlier valuation ($29.0B) significantly distorts the upper bound, while the lower three peers cluster between $150M and $500M.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a median of $400M. The $120M spread reflects uncertainty around comparable-company selection and the substantial variance within the peer set. Given that one comparable (Scale AI) trades at a multi-billion-dollar premium relative to peers, investors should weight the lower subcohort of $150M–$500M more heavily for relative valuation purposes. Key uncertainties include DefSecIntel's specific revenue traction, customer concentration, and competitive positioning relative to Stark Defence and Vatn Systems, none of which are quantified in available data.
